name: leads-core-public-api
description: src/lib/leads (import from @/lib/leads barrel) is the Phase-2 wave-1 foundation the OTP, inquiry-submission, seller-inbox, and buyer-account builders depend on. Exports: inquirySchema (Zod v4 inquiry payload), the lead status machine (canTransition/assertTransition, LEAD_TRANSITIONS), LeadDTO + serialize(row), and newMetaEventId(). Rate limiting lives in @/lib/rate-limit and CAPTCHA in @/lib/captcha.

name: rate-limit-injectable-store
description: Redis sliding-window limiter (src/lib/rate-limit) depends on a narrow RateLimitRedis interface (zremrangebyscore/zadd/zcard/pexpire/zrangeWithScoresAsc), NOT ioredis directly. Production uses ioRedisStore(getRedisConnection()); unit tests inject an in-memory fake so no network/ephemeral Redis is needed. limit() runs the commands sequentially (not MULTI) — safe because each call awaits its own zadd before its own zcard, so it can never under-count and wrongly admit. Fails CLOSED by default on a Redis outage (lead submission), fail-open is opt-in.

title: Lead status machine models the lost sink with a multi-source transition
rationale: The listings machine gives each named action a single from→to edge. Leads deviate: LEAD_TRANSITIONS.from is a readonly LeadStatus[] so the single 'lose' action fires from new/contacted/negotiating. Rationale: 'lost' is a sink reachable from every live stage; enumerating lose_new/lose_contacted/lose_negotiating would be noise. Forward progress stays strictly one-step (no skip to won, no reopening from won/lost). ALLOWED adjacency and transitionAction() are still derived from the table, preserving the listings pattern's single-source-of-truth guarantee.

content: CAPTCHA verify (src/lib/captcha, Cloudflare Turnstile) is env-guarded exactly like email (Resend) and phone-OTP (Twilio): with no TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY it no-ops to success:true skipped:true so dev/test never hit the network; with a secret it verifies and FAILS CLOSED (success:false) on any network/parse error or non-OK HTTP. Server helper uses TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY (never logged); the public NEXT_PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY belongs to the wave-2 client widget. Neither env var is in .env.example yet — add before enabling in prod.

name: lead-first-view-idempotent-stamp
description: Seller inbox stamps seller_first_viewed_at with a single UPDATE ... SET seller_first_viewed_at=now() WHERE id=? AND seller_id=? AND seller_first_viewed_at IS NULL, then falls back to a plain owned SELECT when 0 rows updated. The IS NULL guard makes 'first read' idempotent (a re-read never moves the timestamp) and the fallback keeps repeat GETs at 200. GET /api/dashboard/leads/[id] is the read that stamps; the collection GET never stamps so unread leads keep their funnel signal until opened.

title: Seller inbox scopes leads by denormalized seller_id alone
rationale: listSellerLeads filters on leads.seller_id only (backed by leads_seller_id_created_at_idx), never joining listings.deleted_at. A lead outlives a soft-deleted listing and must stay visible/actionable in the inbox, and the denormalized seller_id is the hottest seller query (spec §6). Ownership on GET/PATCH [id] is also seller_id-based; a non-owned lead resolves to null → 404 (existence never disclosed as 403), mirroring the listings ownership-404 convention.

content: Leads have no admin/worker transition split (unlike listings): every lead status move is a seller action, so the [id] PATCH route calls leads-core assertTransition directly with no seller-policy layer (no transitions.ts). The UI mirror leadActionsFor() offers allowedTransitions() as-is. Also: dashboard leads UI components live co-located under src/app/dashboard/leads/_components (not src/components/dashboard) because that dir was outside this feature's file scope — an underscore _components folder is route-excluded and keeps the feature self-contained.

name: inquiry-route-pipeline
description: POST /api/listings/[id]/leads gate order: requireActor (401) -> enforceInquiryRateLimits early to shield the DB (fail-closed 429) -> parseSubmission strips honeypot+captchaToken BEFORE the leads-core .strict() schema (strict rejects unknown keys, so envelope fields must be peeled off first) -> loadActiveListing (404) -> assertNotSelfInquiry (403) -> assertPhoneVerified via users.phone_verified_at (403) -> verifyCaptcha LAST so its network call only fires for otherwise-valid inquiries -> createLead+lead_count bump in one tx (meta_event_id minted, consent_at set) -> enqueue seller+buyer emails best-effort. Pure _lib modules (http/ip/guards/submission/rate-limit) are unit-tested; service.ts (DB/queue) is thin and untested, matching the admin-moderation convention.

Merged main could not boot: Next.js rejects sibling dynamic route segments with different param names, so wave-1 GET /api/listings/[slug] collided with wave-2 POST /api/listings/[id]/leads. Every request 500'd with "You cannot use different slug names for the same dynamic path ('id' !== 'slug')". Critically, 'next build' SUCCEEDS and lists both routes — only the runtime router (next start) throws — so static gates (build/typecheck/lint/test) all pass and the defect is invisible without an integration boot. The AI-merged conflict resolution only touched mount-points.tsx, never this collision.
resolution: Unify the public listings subtree on [slug] (matches spec §7 and /land/[slug]): move the leads route to src/app/api/listings/[slug]/leads, resolve the listing by listings.slug in loadActiveListing (it still returns the row id for the lead insert), and have the inquiry form POST to /api/listings/[slug]/leads via listing.slug (InquiryPanel already had slug). Lesson: any wave that adds /api/listings/[X]/... MUST reuse the exact [slug] segment name; a real 'next start' boot + /api/health probe is the only gate that catches sibling-segment collisions.

name: lead-lifecycle-e2e-driver
description: scripts/verify-lead-lifecycle.ts drives the full Phase-2 lead+lists lifecycle against a REAL running 'next start' + Postgres + a booted BullMQ worker (43 assertions): dev-mode phone OTP (fixed code 000000, no Twilio) -> users.phone_verified_at set -> inquiry -> lead row (consent_at + meta_event_id + status new) -> 2 emails enqueued AND drained by the worker (assert getJobCounts completed delta >=2) -> anon 401 / unverified 403 / self-inquiry 403 / 20-burst 429 -> seller inbox scoping + status machine (409 on illegal jump) -> buyer My-Inquiries + saved-list price_at_save. Two repeatability keys: (1) it deletes rl:* rate-limit keys at startup because the demo seller and the shared 127.0.0.1 IP are reused across runs and their windows leak 429s into the self-inquiry/burst assertions; (2) Better Auth rate-limits its own /sign-up|/sign-in endpoints, so the auth helpers back off and retry on 429. My-Inquiries returns {lead,listing} pairs (id is at .lead.id, not .id).
